name: Communication
description_short: Communication Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving Oklahoma and Kansas since 1939, offering checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, and personal loans with competitive rates.
description_long:
Communication Federal Credit Union (CFCU) has operated as a member-owned financial cooperative since 1939, establishing itself as a stable institution in Oklahoma and Kansas. The organization is federally insured by the NCUA and holds a 30-year history of receiving Bauer Financial Reports' Five-Star rating, indicating strong capital reserves and operational stability. As a not-for-profit credit union, CFCU returns profits to members rather than external shareholders, which directly influences its pricing and service model.

CFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including deposit accounts (PayMe Checking with cash-back rewards), certificates of deposit (currently featuring 4.10% APY on 9-month terms and 3.85% APY on 13-month terms), auto loans (starting at 5.49% APR), personal loans for consolidation or major purchases, mortgages, youth savings accounts, and 24/7 digital banking through online platforms and mobile app. The institution emphasizes personalized service, with staff trained to know members individually rather than treating them as account numbers.

CFCU differentiates itself through its cooperative structure, which theoretically enables lower fees and better rates than traditional banks due to profit-sharing with members. The institution has maintained consistent Five-Star ratings for three decades and emphasizes a relationship-based approach to member service. Digital tools include Zelle money transfers, bill pay, real-time alerts, debit card controls, and access to free credit reports and scores.

While CFCU appears to be a legitimate, well-capitalized credit union with strong regulatory standing, potential members should note that membership is likely restricted to specific employer groups or geographic areas (as is typical for credit unions), and the website does not clearly state eligibility requirements. Rates advertised (5.49% auto, 4.10% certificate) are competitive but not exceptional in current market conditions. The lack of detailed fee schedules and product terms on the homepage requires contacting the institution for specifics.
services: PayMe Checking accounts with cash-back rewards on debit card purchases and no monthly fees
Certificate of deposit products (9-month and 13-month terms with fixed APY rates)
Auto loans with rates starting at 5.49% APR for dealership and private-party purchases
Personal loans for large purchases, debt consolidation, and unexpected expenses
Mortgage loans for home purchases and refinancing
Youth savings accounts to teach children financial habits
Online banking platform with account management and balance checking
CFCU mobile app for on-the-go account access and management
Bill pay and Zelle money transfer services
Debit card controls including block/unblock functionality and real-time alerts
Free credit report and credit score access for members
pros: Not-for-profit structure returns profits to members as better rates, lower fees, and improved services rather than to external shareholders
Consistent 30-year history of Five-Star ratings from Bauer Financial Reports, indicating strong financial stability and capital reserves
Federally insured by NCUA with accounts protected, plus claims of being among the safest financial institutions in the country
PayMe Checking offers rewards cash back on debit card purchases with no monthly fees
24/7 digital access including mobile app, Zelle transfers, bill pay, real-time alerts, and free credit score/report access
Personalized service model emphasizing member relationships rather than treating customers as account numbers
Wide range of loan products including auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and youth savings with stated commitment to helping members achieve financial goals
cons: Membership eligibility and requirements are not clearly stated on the website, suggesting potential restrictions on who can join (typical for credit unions but not transparent here)
Advertised rates (5.49% APR auto, 4.10% APY certificate) are competitive but not exceptional compared to current market offerings from banks and online lenders
Website does not provide detailed fee schedules, product terms, APR/APY ranges, or loan approval requirements, requiring members to contact directly for specifics
Limited information about personal loan terms, amounts, or eligibility criteria on the homepage
No mention of business accounts, business loans, or investment services, limiting utility for small business owners
best_for: Members eligible to join CFCU who prioritize relationship-based banking and member-owned profit-sharing over bank services
Auto buyers seeking competitive rates with straightforward application and pre-approval processes
Savers looking for competitive certificate rates in a federally insured, highly-rated institution with long-term stability
Individuals seeking personalized financial guidance and service from staff trained to know members individually
diagnosis: Communication Federal Credit Union is best suited for individuals eligible for membership who value a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution with proven long-term stability and personalized service over purely rate-driven shopping. The main caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted (typical for credit unions), and detailed product terms and fee information require direct contact rather than being available on the public website.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Communication — OKLAHOMA CITY, OK Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: Communication — NCUA-insured credit union in OKLAHOMA CITY, OK. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
